title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Colin discusses the investigation of Rackin with Hakast and Dick. Colin explains that Rackin’s activities involve a sophisticated organization between the Portsmouth Naval Base and London, driven by a motive of money rather than political ideals. Colin admits to using Rackin to leak valuable intelligence. Colin also reveals his personal investigation into "New Moon Street" and various moon-themed pubs and crescent-shaped streets in Portbury, motivated by a sketch found in the wallet of a deceased boy named Hambley. The sketch contains the number "61" and the letter "M." Dick informs Colin that number 61 on Wilbraham New Moon Street is occupied by an architect and offers to help Colin investigate the area and check for foreign residents the following morning.

The next day, Hakast is angry because a leather travel clock marked "Rosemary" has gone missing from a collection of clocks. Hakast explains that during the process of moving a body and packing the clocks, a subordinate named Edward reported only finding three clocks instead of four. Colin recalls that Miss Pemahyu briefly returned to the house alone to retrieve forgotten gloves. Hakast suggests that she may have taken the clock and implies that Colin is being too protective of her due to romantic feelings. Hakast warns Colin that the girl is deeply involved in the murder case and has her own problems.
